topic: liqueur de tirage and secondary fermentation

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A measured mixture of wine, sugar, yeast, yeast nutrients, and a clarifying agent is added to the base-wine blend.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The added yeast ferments the added sugar in the sealed bottle, producing alcohol and carbon dioxide.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The second fermentation raises alcohol slightly and traps dissolved carbon dioxide, creating pressure and sparkle.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The second fermentation raises alcohol slightly and traps dissolved carbon dioxide, creating pressure and sparkle.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
